title: `--coverage` triggers complaints for certain ternary assignments.
body:
Hi team at Verilator!

In version 5.042, we noticed multiple new warnings like the following:
```
%Warning-SIDEEFFECT: top.sv:36:67: Expression side effect may be mishandled
                                 : ... Suggest use a temporary variable in place of this expression
   36 | assign out_ipv4_header.header_chk_sum = csum == 16'hFFFF ? csum : ~csum;
      |                                                                   ^
                     ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/SIDEEFFECT?v=5.042
                     ... Use "/* verilator lint_off SIDEEFFECT */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
```

To reproduce the issue, please clone this repo.:
[https://github.com/etep/verilator-5.042-possible-issue](https://github.com/etep/verilator-5.042-possible-issue)

Run `make` or `make clean && make`.

If arg. `--coverage` is removed, the warnings are gone.
code_blocks:
%Warning-SIDEEFFECT: top.sv:36:67: Expression side effect may be mishandled
                                 : ... Suggest use a temporary variable in place of this expression
   36 | assign out_ipv4_header.header_chk_sum = csum == 16'hFFFF ? csum : ~csum;
      |                                                                   ^
                     ... For warning description see https://verilator.org/warn/SIDEEFFECT?v=5.042
                     ... Use "/* verilator lint_off SIDEEFFECT */" and lint_on around source to disable this message.
